name: Regional Finance
description_short: Regional Finance is a branch-based personal loan lender with 30+ years in business, serving Houston borrowers including those with less-than-perfect credit.
description_long:
Regional Finance has operated in the consumer lending space for over 30 years. This Houston branch at 1804 Wirt Road serves the local community through a combination of in-branch service, phone applications, and online prequalification. The branch is staffed by local lending specialists and managed by Shudney Crumby, operating Monday through Friday with no weekend hours.

The lender offers a broad portfolio of personal loan products covering a wide range of borrower needs: personal loans, debt consolidation loans, auto-secured loans, auto repair loans, appliance loans, furniture loans, holiday loans, and travel loans. Loan insurance is also available as an add-on. Loan amounts and interest rates are not publicly disclosed on the website; terms are determined individually based on the applicant's credit profile and income. Documents required at application include a government-issued photo ID, proof of residence, and proof of income.

Regional Finance differentiates itself by explicitly welcoming borrowers with less-than-perfect credit — a segment many traditional banks and online prime lenders decline. The prequalification step uses a soft credit pull, allowing applicants to check for offers without any impact to their credit score. The branch model provides face-to-face guidance throughout the application process, which can be valuable for borrowers who are less experienced with lending products or navigating financial stress.

The honest picture is mixed. Regional Finance fills a real gap for subprime borrowers who have limited alternatives, and its 30-year track record suggests stability. However, the lack of published APR ranges is a significant transparency gap — borrowers cannot comparison-shop without completing a hard-pull application. Loan insurance add-ons, which the company promotes, are frequently high-cost relative to their benefit. Weekend access is unavailable, and the final application cannot be completed fully online. Borrowers should compare the full terms carefully before accepting any offer.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
address: 3724 Las Estancias Way Suite D, Albuquerque, NM 87121

company_info.headquarters: 3724 Las Estancias Way Suite D, Albuquerque, NM 87121

pros: Over 30 years of consumer lending experience
Explicitly accepts applicants with less-than-perfect credit
Soft-pull prequalification — check offers without impacting credit score
Broad loan menu: debt consolidation, auto repair, appliances, furniture, travel, holidays
In-person branch service with local lending specialists in Houston
Flexible payment options structured around the borrower's budget
Application available online, by phone, or in-branch
cons: APR ranges and loan amounts not disclosed on the website — must complete application to see terms
Hard credit pull required to finalize the application after prequalification
No fully digital application process — final steps require branch visit or phone call
Branch is closed Saturday and Sunday — no weekend access
Loan insurance add-on product adds cost; value relative to premium is not disclosed
best_for: Borrowers with fair or poor credit who have been declined by banks or online prime lenders
Houston residents needing purpose-specific financing (appliance replacement, auto repair, debt consolidation)
Consumers who prefer in-person guidance and local branch support
Borrowers who want to prequalify first without risking their credit score
diagnosis: Regional Finance Houston is best suited for borrowers with imperfect credit who need a purpose-driven personal loan and value working with a local, in-person lender. The primary caveat is that interest rates are not disclosed upfront, and as a subprime-focused lender the APRs are likely materially higher than prime alternatives — borrowers with good credit should shop elsewhere first.
